rulings.Case Title: HERMAN STARNES VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 12, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Todd Shipyards Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to affirmative defense 22 (sophisticated user) and affirmative defense 15 (state of scientific knowledge) and taken off calendar as to strict liability. Assuming without deciding that some variation of the ¿sophisticated user¿ defense can be asserted by a premises owner/contractor who is sued based on an allegation that it disturbed asbestos rather than supplying an asbestos-containing product, defendant failed to submit evidence sufficient to successfully invoke such a defense. Among other things, defendant failed to establish that defendant ensured that plaintiff, either directly or through his employer, was informed about the hazards of the asbestos-disturbing activities conducted by defendant that were known or should have been known to defendant and that plaintiff knew or should have known of the hazards of the asbestos-disturbing activities conducted by defendant. The declaration of Dr. Richard Cohen creates a triable issue whether defendant should have known of the hazards of asbestos during the relevant time period. Defendant¿s objections to Dr. Cohen¿s declaration are overruled. Plainitff represents that he dismissed his claim for strict liability. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES FEDUSKA VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 12, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Pneumo Abex LLC¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff¿s supplemental interrogatory responses alleged exposure based on his use of Rayloc brakes. By failing to ask plaintiff about Rayloc brakes at sessions of plaintiff¿s deposition that took place subsequent to service of the supplemental responses, defendant did not sustain its burden of production. (Scheiding). Even if defendant did sustain its burden of production, plaintiff¿s declaration and Mr. LeCour¿s deposition testimony create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to asbestos-containing Rayloc brakes attributable to defendant. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration and Mr. LeCour¿s deposition testimony are overruled. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES FEDUSKA VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 12, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Advance Mechanical Contractors, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. The declaration of plaintiff and paragraph 8 of the declaration of Mr. Ay create a triable issue whether plaintiff was exposed to irrespirable fibers from asbestos-containing transite pipe disturbed by defendant. Defendant¿s separate statement did not contain any purported undisputed material facts on the issue of whether plaintiff¿s exposure to any asbestos attributable to defendant was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to plaintiff¿s asserted asbestos-related illness, but even if it had done so and defendant satisfied its burden of production on that issue, the declaration of Dr. Bruch created a triable issue. Defendant¿s objections to plaintiff¿s declaration and paragraph 8 of Mr. Ay¿s declaration are overruled. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ADRIAN HOWES et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Co-Defendant Western Building Supplys Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, January 12, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant A.W. Chesterton Co.¿s joinder in co-defendant Western Building Supply's motion to dismiss for failure to prosecute is off calendar. Defendant was dismissed December 6, 2010. = (220/HEK)
